task_completion.description: Set document to landscape orientation with narrow margins
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreOffice Writer page appears wider than it is tall (landscape) and the margins on the rulers appear narrow (0.5 inch).

task_completion.description: Create a 2x2 table filling most of the page
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2x2 grid/table is visible in the document, scaled to take up the majority of the visible page area.

task_completion.description: Configure the correct background colors for each cell
task_completion.visual_evidence: The table cells have the following background colors: Top-Left is Red, Top-Right is Yellow, Bottom-Left is Blue, Bottom-Right is Green.

task_completion.description: Insert the correct text into each corresponding quadrant
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'CODE RED' is in the Top-Left, 'CODE YELLOW' in the Top-Right, 'CODE BLUE' in the Bottom-Left, and 'ALL CLEAR' in the Bottom-Right, along with their respective descriptions.

task_completion.description: Format text with centering and larger bold headings
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text in all four cells is centered both horizontally and vertically. The 'CODE ...' headings are bolded and visibly larger than the descriptive body text below them.

task_completion.description: Ensure text color is readable against the backgrounds
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text color is White in the Red, Blue, and Green cells. The text color is Black in the Yellow cell.
The title bar of LibreOffice Writer shows 'emergency_cards.docx' or a save dialog is visible saving the file with this name in the Documents folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Writer GUI to create the document
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreOffice Writer application open and being interacted with (menus, toolbars, typing).

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal scripts to bypass GUI document creation
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing Python scripts (e.g., python-docx) or pandoc commands being used to generate the .docx file directly.

integrity.description: The agent sourced the content from the provided text file
integrity.visual_evidence: The file 'card_content.txt' is seen open in a text editor or terminal (via cat/less) to read or copy the descriptions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is /home/ga/Documents/emergency_cards.docx. The content must be sourced from /home/ga/Documents/card_content.txt. The four quadrants must contain the following headings: Top-Left: 'CODE RED', Top-Right: 'CODE YELLOW', Bottom-Left: 'CODE BLUE', Bottom-Right: 'ALL CLEAR'.
